# Hermes Desktop Setup for Automatic Task Feeding

**Goal:** Your Hermes Desktop app (already open on your Mac) automatically receives and opens new "Hermes task" files generated by the Forge (e.g., when you ask the in-app Grok chat on the live site to polish a BJJ card or equipment).

This way, you get deep Hermes processing with almost zero manual work – just keep the app running.

## Current Understanding
- Hermes Desktop can be launched from Terminal.
- It's currently open on your machine.
- Tasks are .md files created in your Obsidian vault at: `00 Meta/Hermes Tasks/`

## Next: Tell Me These Details (Reply With Answers)
To make the watcher script work perfectly for you:

1. Exact Terminal command that launches Hermes Desktop (e.g., `open -a "Hermes Desktop"`, or `hermes-desktop`, or something else). Test in Terminal if unsure.

2. Does opening a .md file with Hermes Desktop work to load a task? (Test: In Terminal, run `open -a "Hermes Desktop" /path/to/a-sample-task.md` and see if it loads in the app.)

3. Confirm the full path to your Hermes Tasks folder (default is `/Users/darrenjorgenson/Obsidian/Jorgenson Brain/00 Meta/Hermes Tasks` – is this exact?).

## Once You Reply
I'll:
- Customize the watcher script with your exact launch command.
- Give you 2–3 simple Terminal commands to set it up as a background process (so it runs even if you close Terminal).
- Test command to create a sample task and watch it auto-open in Hermes Desktop.

This pairs perfectly with the live site chat: Ask Grok for a complex update → it creates the task in the vault → watcher on your Mac auto-feeds it to Hermes Desktop → you review in Hermes → paste response back into chat if needed for apply.

No Telegram required. Fully local + automated.

Reply with the 3 answers above, and we'll set it up in <5 minutes of your time.
